existing_plugin_systems.description: Canonical plugin API for OpenCode. Defines Plugin (server-side hooks), PluginModule, PluginInput, Hooks interface with 15+ hook points (chat.message, chat.params, shell.env, tool.definition, tool.execute.before/after, experimental.chat.messages.transform, permission.ask, etc.)

TUI plugin system with slot-based UI extension (TuiSlotMap, TuiSlotPlugin), route registration, keymap/command binding, dialog stack, theme API, attention/notification system, sound packs, and KV storage per plugin

Plugin loader and lifecycle manager. Loads internal auth plugins (Codex, Copilot, GitLab, Poe, Cloudflare, Azure, DigitalOcean, xAI) and external npm/local plugins. Orchestrates hook registration via PluginLoader, bus subscription, and init/config/dispose lifecycle

Plugin resolution and package management: npm-package-arg parsing, package.json traversal, entry point resolution for server/tui kinds, compatibility checking, theme file discovery via oc-themes field

Config-driven plugin declaration (plugin field in opencode.json). Supports Spec as string or [string, Options] tuple. Scope tracking (global vs local), provenance deduplication, auto-discovery in .opencode/plugin(s)/

Tool registry that integrates plugin-contributed tools (from @opencode-ai/plugin/tool ToolDefinition), wraps them in the internal tool system, and provides them to the LLM prompt via tool.definition hook
Example of a local plugin tool using the @opencode-ai/plugin SDK

effect_di_patterns.description: Generic ConfigService pattern: creates a Context.Service class with typed config fields, auto-generating defaultLayer (from ConfigProvider) and layer (from literal input). Used by RuntimeFlags, DatabaseConfig, DuckDBConfig, and many others
effect_di_patterns.how_it_could_help: Desktop customization settings could use this pattern for type-safe, composable configuration layers sourced from env vars, electron-store, or JSON files

effect_di_patterns.description: RuntimeFlags service with 30+ flags driven by OPENCODE_* env vars. Uses ConfigService pattern with bool(), positiveInteger(), enabledByExperimental() helpers. Supports umbrella experimental flag with individual overrides
effect_di_patterns.how_it_could_help: Desktop features could adopt this pattern for feature gating and experimental flags without env var pollution — sourced from electron-store instead

effect_di_patterns.description: Layer-based plugin construction: Plugin.Service is a Context.Service with typed Interface. defaultLayer composes Bus, Config, and RuntimeFlags layers
effect_di_patterns.how_it_could_help: A desktop customization service could follow the same Layer composition pattern for dependency injection

effect_di_patterns.description: Massive Layer composition example: merges 25+ service layers (Plugin, Config, Database, LSP, Git, Bus, etc.) into one defaultLayer
effect_di_patterns.how_it_could_help: Shows how to compose many services. Desktop customization could plug into this layer composition

effect_di_patterns.description: InstanceState — scoped state management for Effect services. Used by Plugin and other services to maintain lifecycle-scoped mutable state
effect_di_patterns.how_it_could_help: Desktop customization modules could use InstanceState for scoped lifecycle management

desktop_specific_patterns.description: ElectronAPI interface — the full typed IPC bridge between renderer and main process. Defines 40+ methods for window management, file dialogs, store access, zoom, deep links, WSL, updates, etc.
desktop_specific_patterns.pattern: IPC bridge gateway pattern. Every new renderer capability requires: preload handler, IPC handler registration, and type definition here

desktop_specific_patterns.description: IPC handler registration with Deps dependency injection object. All handlers are registered centrally via registerIpcHandlers(deps)
desktop_specific_patterns.pattern: Dependency injection via Deps object. New customization handlers would add methods to Deps type and implement in the handler map

desktop_specific_patterns.description: electron-store wrapper with named store cache. Settings are persisted as JSON files in app.getPath('userData')
desktop_specific_patterns.pattern: Named store pattern. Multiple named stores can be created for different domains

desktop_specific_patterns.description: Constants for store keys (SETTINGS_STORE, DEFAULT_SERVER_URL_KEY, WSL_ENABLED_KEY, PINCH_ZOOM_ENABLED_KEY)
BrowserWindow management (createMainWindow, createLoadingWindow, setTitlebar, setPinchZoomEnabled, zoom, recovery). Window config is hardcoded
desktop_specific_patterns.pattern: Window creation is procedural. Custom window options (titlebar, dimensions, behavior) are not configurable without modifying this file

desktop_specific_patterns.description: Application menu constructed from declarative DESKTOP_MENU config in packages/app/src/desktop-menu.ts. Platform-aware filtering
desktop_specific_patterns.pattern: Declarative menu config drives native Electron menu. Command actions dispatch to renderer via IPC commands or to main via DesktopMenuAction enum

desktop_specific_patterns.description: DesktopMenuAction enum implementation: 18 platform actions (app.checkForUpdates, window.new, view.reload, edit.undo, etc.)
desktop_specific_patterns.pattern: Action dispatcher switch statement pattern. Adding new actions requires extending the DesktopMenuAction type and the switch

desktop_specific_patterns.description: Sidecar process management (utilityProcess.fork). Settings like WSL, default server URL stored in electron-store. Sidecar env is built from parent process env
desktop_specific_patterns.pattern: Sidecar process with IPC message protocol and health checking

desktop_specific_patterns.description: contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('api', api). Maps all ElectronAPI methods to ipcRenderer.invoke/send calls
desktop_specific_patterns.pattern: Strict context isolation. Renderer can only access desktop via typed window.api bridge

desktop_specific_patterns.description: electron-vite build config with custom Vite plugins for node-pty, server module, and asset copying

config_driven_ui.description: Theme system: JSON theme files loaded via import.meta.glob, CSS variable rendering, LocalStorage persistence, plugin-contributed themes via oc-themes, registerTheme() API on the context
config_driven_ui.pattern: JSON-driven theme pipeline. Themes are JSON files → resolved to CSS variables → injected into :root. localStorage caches non-built-in themes

config_driven_ui.description: Feature-rich settings context with 7 subsections (general, updates, appearance, keybinds, permissions, notifications, sounds). Persisted via persisted('settings.v3', ...) utility
config_driven_ui.pattern: createSimpleContext + persisted() store for reactive settings with LocalStorage backing. All settings have typed interfaces and getter/setter methods

config_driven_ui.description: Command/keybind system: command registration, keybind parsing, key matching, palette. Custom keybinds stored in settings
config_driven_ui.pattern: Registration-based extensibility for commands. Components register Commands with ids, titles, keybinds, and callbacks

config_driven_ui.description: Platform abstraction: PlatformProvider with 30+ methods abstracted behind Union type (desktop/web). Desktop renderer provides implementation via window.api
config_driven_ui.pattern: Abstraction layer that lets the app code be platform-agnostic. Desktop renders the Platform interface via Electron API, web via browser APIs

config_driven_ui.description: AppBaseProviders and AppInterface: nested provider tree with 15+ context providers wrapping the app
config_driven_ui.pattern: Provider tree orchestration pattern. Each UI domain has a provider that manages its state and exposes it via context

config_driven_ui.description: Slot-based UI system for TUI: named insertion points (app, app_bottom, home_logo, home_prompt, sidebar_title, sidebar_content, etc.)
config_driven_ui.pattern: Slot pattern for UI extensibility. Components can register slot plugins that render at named positions in the UI tree

env_var_configs.usage: Build channel selection (dev/beta/prod) for desktop app. Sets app name, ID, updater behavior
Override the local server port. Defaults to a random available port
Database path. Special value :memory: for in-memory mode. Absolute path otherwise
Full database connection URL. If set, skips migration. Used for remote database support
Client type discriminator ('cli' | 'desktop'). Defaults to 'cli'. Desktop sets it in server.ts preferAppEnv()
Disable all built-in auth plugins (Codex, Copilot, etc.) for testing
Skip all external plugins. Set by --pure flag on CLI
Umbrella flag that enables all experimental features. Individual flags can override it
30+ per-feature experimental flags (BACKGROUND_SUBAGENTS, LSP_TOOL, PLAN_MODE, EVENT_SYSTEM, WORKSPACES, ICON_DISCOVERY, DUCKDB_QUERY, OXFMT, ENABLE_EXA, etc.)
When '1', creates temporary directory structure and uses :memory: DB for onboarding testing
Sentry error tracking for renderer process
Used by app settings to determine if newLayoutDesigns should default to true (non-prod channels)
Dev server URL for hot reload. When set, main process loads renderer from this URL instead of the custom oc:// protocol
Sentry Vite plugin configuration for source map upload

i18n_as_customization_model.how: The i18n system in packages/desktop/src/renderer/i18n/ and packages/app/src/i18n/ uses a merge pattern: base (English) flattened dict, then locale-specific overrides via i18n.flatten(). The same merge pattern could be used for customization: a base configuration merged with user-provided customization files. The @solid-primitives/i18n translator function pattern (t(key, params)) could be adapted to a customization lookup function. Theme customization already partially uses this model (theme JSON files merged into a theme store via import.meta.glob). Desktop customization could extend this to UI layout, component registry, and plugin contributions via the same merge + flatten approach.

missing: No slot system for UI injection
No plugin system for renderer extensibility

customization_options: IPC handler registration via Deps DI
missing: No plugin loading in main process

recommendation: Best pattern to borrow for desktop customization: the TUI slot system (TuiSlotPlugin / TuiSlotMap) adapted to the Electron/SolidJS renderer, combined with the plugin loading infrastructure (PluginLoader + Config-driven plugin spec) from the opencode core. The desktop renderer should gain a slot-based plugin system where external plugins can register React/SolidJS components at named insertion points. Plugins should be specified in opencode.json, loaded via the existing PluginLoader, and contribute hooks/UI via a desktop-specific interface similar to TuiPlugin but for Electron (DesktopPlugin). The existing theme system (JSON files → CSS variables) and i18n merge pattern provide proven patterns for customization file loading and merging.
